_comment: Cross-version runtime x SDK compatibility matrix for AAASM-3157.
Each entry pairs one aa-runtime channel/version with one language-SDK
version and records whether that combination is a SUPPORTED integration
target. Every row carries a reason string; unsupported rows must explain
WHY the pair is not validated (e.g. the SDK predates a wire-protocol change).
Channels: 'latest-branch' tracks main HEAD, 'latest-release' is the most
recent published release, 'previous-release' is the prior supported release
This data is consumed offline by tests/compat/matrix.py; the live build of
any single combination is opt-in and skip-guarded (cargo/protoc + SDK
toolchains are not present in CI/offline sandboxes).

entries.reason: main x main is the primary development integration target.
main x main is the primary development integration target.
main x main is the primary development integration target.
Latest published release: runtime and python-sdk ship at the same tag.
Latest published release: runtime and node-sdk ship at the same tag.
Latest published release: runtime and go-sdk ship at the same tag (go uses v-prefix).
Previous supported release: prior alpha kept under compatibility validation (AC4).
Previous supported release: prior alpha kept under compatibility validation (AC4).
Previous supported release: prior alpha kept under compatibility validation (AC4).
Unsupported: alpha.1 python-sdk predates the main wire-protocol change; an old SDK against a new runtime is not validated.
Unsupported: a newer node-sdk release against an older runtime release is not a validated upgrade path.
